messages.content:
You are an expert Technical Product Manager.
Your job is to analyze the user's issue (Title and Body) along with the provided source code context, and generate a User Story, Acceptance Criteria, and Tasks.

OUTPUT FORMAT REQUIREMENTS:
You MUST output ONLY a valid JSON object. Do not wrap it in markdown. Do not include any explanations outside of the JSON.
The JSON must strictly follow this structure:
{
  "title": "Short title of the story",
  "story_type": "software_feature", 
  "jira_issue_type": "Story",
  "jira_labels": ["backend", "frontend", "bug", etc],
  "jira_linked_items": [],
  "user_story": "As a [role], I want to [action] so that [benefit].",
  "acceptance_criteria": [
    "Given [precondition] When [action] Then [result]"
  ],
  "story_points": null, // Hoặc một số nguyên Fibonacci (1, 2, 3, 5, 8) đánh giá độ khó
  "tasks": {
    "be": ["Backend task 1", "Backend task 2"],
    "fe": ["Frontend task 1"],
    "qa": ["Test case 1"]
  },
  "definition_of_done": ["Code reviewed", "Tests passed"],
  "planning_status": "READY_FOR_SPRINT",
  "clarification_questions": [],
  "assumptions": [],
  "story_splits": [],
  "sprint_allocation": [],
  "context_sources": []
}

If the issue lacks so much context that it's impossible to deduce the required changes even with the provided code context, you may set "planning_status" to "NEEDS_CLARIFICATION" and provide your questions in "clarification_questions", leaving "tasks" empty. Otherwise, strive to generate concrete tasks.

ISSUE TITLE: Agent host: workspace is attached as context ISSUE BODY: repro steps: 1. send a request 2. exit the session 3. revisit the same session 4. happens in both editor and agents window --- RAG CONTEXT (SOURCE CODE) --- --- File: vscode_ai_docs_full.md --- ## `AgentSessionAdapter` — Wrapping Existing Sessions Adapts an existing `IAgentSession` from the chat layer into the `ISession` facade: - Constructs with initial values from the agent session's metadata and timing - `update(session)` performs a batched observable transaction to update all reactive properties - Extracts workspace info, changes, description, and GitHub info from session metadata - Maps `ChatSessionStatus` → `SessionStatus` - Handles both CLI and Cloud session metadata formats for repository resolution --- File: vscode_ai_docs_full.md --- # Agent Host The agent host communicates via the Agent Host Protocol. The specification for this lives in a directory `../agent-host-protocol` as a sibling of the VS Code directory. If this directory doesn't exist, you should use the "ask questions" tool to ask the user if they want to clone `git@github.com:microsoft/agent-host-protocol.git` to that directory. After doing so, you should also prompt the user to add `file:////plugins/copilot-plugin` as a plugin in their `chat.pluginLocations` settings. --- File: vscode_ai_docs_full.md --- ### 5. Subagent Session Support Sessions may contain subagents - parallel task executions spawned via the Task tool. These are stored in: ``` {workspace-slug}/{session-id}/subagents/agent-{id}.jsonl ``` The service automatically: 1. Discovers subagent directories for each session 2. Parses `agent-*.jsonl` files within them 3. Attaches subagent sessions to their parent via `session.subagents` Each `SubagentSession` contains: - `agentId`: The short hex identifier (e.g., "a139fcf") - `messages`: The subagent's conversation history - `timestamp`: When the subagent last had activity --- File: vscode_ai_docs_full.md --- # Agent Host Sessions Provider **Folder:** `src/vs/sessions/contrib/providers/agentHost/` The agent host provider family backs sessions run by an **agent host** — an out-of-process (or in-process) agent runtime that exposes one or more agents (Copilot, Codex, Claude, …) over the agent host protocol (`platform/agentHost`). It is the largest provider in the Agents window and is shared between the local window and remote hosts: | Class | File | Purpose | |-------|------|---------| | `BaseAgentHostSessionsProvider` | `browser/baseAgentHostSessionsProvider.ts` | Abstract base implementing the full `ISessionsProvider` surface against an `IAgentConnection`. ~2700 lines; contains `AgentHostSessionAdapter` (the `ISession` impl) and `NewSession` (pre-creation draft). | | `LocalAgentHostSessionsProvider` | `browser/localAgentHostSessionsProvider.ts` | Concrete local-window provider backed by the in-process `IAgentHostService`. | | `RemoteAgentHostSessionsProvider` | `../remoteAgentHost/` | Concrete remote provider (one per connection). Documented separately in [`REMOTE_AGENT_HOST_SESSIONS_PROVIDER.md`](../remoteAgentHost/REMOTE_AGENT_HOST_SESSIONS_PROVIDER.md). | This document covers the shared base and the **local** concrete provider. For the remote variant, read the remote doc — it extends the same base. --- File: vscode_ai_docs_full.md --- # Agent host system-prompt customization This directory customizes the system prompt for Copilot CLI **agent host** (ahp+cli) sessions. Read this before changing how the system message is built or adding per-model / per-tool guidance. It mirrors the Copilot extension's `extensions/copilot/.../prompts/node/agent/` (agentPrompts), but the agent host runs in its own process and cannot use prompt-tsx, so contributors return plain data the SDK accepts directly.

response:
```json
{
  "title": "Fix: Workspace context not attached when revisiting agent host sessions",
  "story_type": "software_feature", 
  "jira_issue_type": "Bug",
  "jira_labels": ["agent-host", "session-management", "workspace-context", "bug"],
  "jira_linked_items": [],
  "user_story": "As a developer using the Agent Host, I want workspace context to be properly attached when revisiting an existing session so that my previous work environment and context is restored correctly in both the editor and agents windows.",
  "acceptance_criteria": [
    "Given a previously exited agent host session with workspace context, When I revisit the same session, Then the workspace should be attached as context in both the editor window and agents window",
    "Given an agent host session with subagents, When I exit and revisit the session, Then parent workspace context remains intact and is properly restored",
    "Given a new agent host session created after revisiting, When I open it, Then it should not have stale/incorrect workspace context from previous sessions",
    "The workspace attachment state must persist correctly across session lifecycle (create → exit → revisit)"
  ],
  "story_points": 5,
  "tasks": {
    "be": [
      "Investigate AgentSessionAdapter to understand how workspace metadata is extracted and stored during session creation",
      "Trace the session update mechanism in BaseAgentHostSessionsProvider to identify where workspace context might be lost on revisit",
      "Add logging to track workspace attachment state at: session creation, exit, and revisit points",
      "Fix the session recreation logic to properly restore workspace context from persisted metadata",
      "Ensure subagent sessions don't interfere with parent workspace attachment during recreation"
    ],
    "fe": [
      "Verify that both editor window and agents window correctly display/receive the attached workspace context on session revisit",
      "Add UI feedback when workspace context is successfully restored after revisiting a session",
      "Ensure any stale workspace state is cleared before attaching new context"
    ],
    "qa": [
      "Create test case: Exit agent host session, verify workspace context is detached, then revisit and confirm it's reattached",
      "Test subagent scenario: Verify parent workspace remains attached when revisiting sessions with active subagents",
      "Verify that new sessions created after revisit don't inherit stale workspace state from previous sessions"
    ]
  },
  "definition_of_done": [
    "Bug is fixed and verified in both editor and agents windows",
    "Unit tests added for session recreation and workspace context restoration",
    "Integration tests covering subagent + parent workspace scenario",
    "Code reviewed by team lead"
  ],
  "planning_status": "READY_FOR_SPRINT",
  "clarification_questions": [
    "What specific aspect of 'workspace attached as context' is broken? Is it the metadata not persisting, or the UI not displaying it?",
    "Are there any known sessions that exhibit this behavior vs others that work correctly?",
    "Is this reproducible consistently across different agent host configurations (local vs remote)?"
  ],
  "assumptions": [
    "The issue is specifically about workspace context being lost/restored incorrectly during session exit and revisit",
    "Session metadata containing workspace information should persist to disk or be cached",
    "Both editor and agents windows share the same underlying session object"
  ],
  "story_splits": [],
  "sprint_allocation": [2],
  "context_sources": ["vscode_ai_docs_full.md - AgentSessionAdapter section", "vscode_ai_docs_full.md - Subagent Session Support section", "vscode_ai_docs_full.md - Agent Host Sessions Provider table"]
}
```
